description: Documentation cleanup and GitHub Actions test CI - first evolving PRD slice

finalSuccessCriteria.description: Ralph CLI documentation is clearer for install/run/setup, and the repository has GitHub Actions CI that runs the available test or verification commands for the CLI and related packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Repository documentation clearly explains the current install, setup, skill, and run workflows without stale two-skill or npm-placeholder confusion
Documentation describes the Node CLI path and manual shell script path accurately
A GitHub Actions workflow exists under .github/workflows and runs the repository's available unit-test or verification commands
The CI workflow covers the CLI package verification commands
If no true unit tests exist yet, the workflow documents and runs the strongest available checks such as typecheck and build
All configured local verification commands pass

planning.currentObjective: Explore the repository's documentation, package layout, and available verification commands before changing docs or CI

prdChain.objective: Explore current docs and test/build commands, then record a plan for docs cleanup and GitHub Actions CI

userStories.title: Explore docs and verification commands
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Ralph to inspect the repository documentation and available package checks before planning edits so that the next PRD slice is grounded in the actual repo.
acceptanceCriteria: Review README.md and cli/README.md for stale or confusing install, setup, skill, and run instructions
Inspect package.json files to identify available verification commands for the CLI and flowchart packages
Inspect whether .github/workflows already exists and note any existing CI patterns
Append an exploration summary to progress.txt with recommended next slice for documentation cleanup and GitHub Actions CI
Do not edit documentation or add CI in this exploration story
Mark US-001 as passing only after the exploration summary is recorded
userStories.notes: Exploration complete. See progress.txt for findings and next slice recommendations.
